name: rtx
_comment_rtx: RTX 5060 Ti (sm_120, 16 GB) on host. Mixed (prefill+decode) primary. COMBINED-mode head: when MultiEngineRouter picks a 2-GPU plan, it borrows rtx3060 as the expert-peer. The combined_ot_split regex is the ik/llama.cpp convention for routing FFN expert tensors to the peer's ggml RPC backend; the actual engine-side wiring is in ddvnguyen/llama.cpp#19/#20. For SOLO requests, rtx runs the Q3_K-mini 35B-A3B on its own (cache_n split 4.5 GB on CUDA0 + 5.0 GB on CUDA1 already proven in the old ablation config; the SOLO path no longer recruits the 3060 unless it's an explicit COMBINED request).

name: rtx3060
_comment_rtx3060: RTX 3060 (sm_86, 12 GB) on host. Mixed (prefill+decode) but lower priority than rtx. Two roles: (a) SOLO — runs its own Q3_K-mini 35B-A3B instance, slower than rtx (~60 tok/s vs ~200 tok/s decode). (b) COMBINED peer — rtx.peer_worker points here, so when a request exceeds MultiEngineThreshold and the engine advertises expert-mode support, this GPU is reserved exclusively for that request. VRAM budget (12 GB) is the limiter; the Q5_K-balanced model does not fit — only the Q3_K-mini can land here.

name: p100
_comment_p100: P100 llama-server runs in single-model mode (--model /mnt/readonly_data/...gguf), not router mode — /models/load returns 404. Leave prefill_model_name / decode_model_name / router_model_name UNSET so CoordinatorConfig.LoadWorkers and Router.PrefillModel/DecodeModel return null and the model_load call is skipped (Router.PrefillModel is gated by `if (m != null)` in WorkerSchedulerService.ModelLoadAsync). The cross-model guard checks the P100's slot model_hash against the stored KV's model_hash on restore (since the P100 runs the same model as the RTX, the hashes match → Proceed). When a Coordinator restart loses the in-memory manifest, the model identity is read from the chunked manifest in the Store (PutManifestAsync writes it alongside n_past / total_size / chunks).
